name: Diaphragmatic Breathing (Belly Breathing)
description: Deep belly breathing to strengthen the diaphragm and promote relaxation.
steps: Sit or lie down in a comfortable position.
Place one hand on your chest and one on your belly.
Breathe in slowly through your nose so that your belly pushes your hand out.
Keep your chest still.
Exhale through pursed lips and let your belly fall.
benefits: Promotes lung efficiency and deep relaxation

name: Pursed Lip Breathing
description: Technique slowing exhalation to improve airway function and oxygenation.
steps: Inhale slowly through your nose for a count of two.
Purse your lips as if whistling.
Exhale gently through pursed lips for a count of four.
benefits: Useful for people with asthma or COPD

name: 4-7-8 Breathing Technique
description: Structured inhale-hold-exhale pattern to induce calm and aid sleep.
steps: Sit with your back straight.
Inhale through the nose for 4 counts.
Hold your breath for 7 counts.
Exhale slowly through the mouth for 8 counts.

name: Box Breathing (Square Breathing)
description: Equal timed inhale, hold, exhale, hold cycle for stress reduction and focus.
steps: Inhale through the nose for 4 counts.
Hold your breath for 4 counts.
Exhale through the mouth for 4 counts.
Hold with empty lungs for 4 counts.
benefits: Used by military and first responders

name: Ujjayi Breathing (Ocean Breath)
description: Gentle throat-constricted breathing used in yoga for calmness and concentration.
steps: Sit upright and relax shoulders.
Inhale slowly through your nose while slightly constricting the throat.
Exhale with the same throat constriction making an ocean sound.
Maintain steady rhythm throughout practice.

name: Anulom Vilom (Alternate Nostril Breathing)
description: Alternate nostril breathing that balances the nervous system and energy flow.
steps: Close your right nostril with your right thumb.
Inhale deeply through left nostril.
Release right nostril, close left with ring finger.
Exhale out right nostril.
Inhale through right, switch fingers, exhale through left.
Continue alternating for 5-10 rounds.
benefits: Balances left and right brain hemispheres

name: Bhastrika (Bellows Breath)
description: Forceful belly breaths that energize the body and strengthen the lungs.
steps: Sit comfortably with your spine erect.
Inhale deeply and forcefully through your nose, expanding your belly.
Exhale forcefully through your nose, contracting your belly.
Repeat brisk inhalations and exhalations for 10–20 cycles.
End with a deep inhale and slow exhale.

name: Bhramari (Humming Bee Breath)
description: Humming exhalation that soothes the mind and reduces anxiety.
steps: Sit in a comfortable position and close your eyes.
Place your thumbs to gently close your ears.
Place remaining fingers over closed eyes.
Inhale deeply through your nose.
Exhale slowly while making a humming sound like a bee.

name: Nadi Shodhana (Channel Cleaning Breath)
description: Selective nostril breathing purifying the energy channels.
steps: Use Vishnu mudra hand position (fold index and middle fingers).
Close right nostril with thumb, inhale through left.
Close both nostrils and retain breath briefly.
Release thumb, exhale through right nostril.
Inhale through right, close both, release left nostril, exhale.
This completes one cycle.

name: Sheetali (Cooling Breath)
description: Cooling breath that lowers body temperature and calms anger.
steps: Sit comfortably with spine straight.
Stick your tongue out and curl the sides up like a tube.
Inhale slowly and deeply through your curled tongue.
Close your mouth and exhale slowly through the nose.

name: Sitkari (Hissing Breath)
description: Hissing breath alternative for cooling and relaxation.
steps: Sit in comfortable position with spine erect.
Part your lips slightly and place tongue against teeth.
Inhale slowly through gaps between teeth making hissing sound.
Close mouth and exhale slowly through nose.
benefits: Alternative to Sheetali for those who can't curl tongue

name: Sitali Breath
description: Tongue-based inhalation for cooling and stress relief.
steps: Sit comfortably and relax shoulders.
Stick tongue out and curl sides to form tube.
Inhale slowly through curled tongue.
Close mouth and exhale through nose.
Repeat 5-10 times.

name: Coherent Breathing (Resonant Breathing)
description: Slow paced breath to optimize heart rate variability and calm.
steps: Sit or lie comfortably with spine aligned.
Breathe in for 5-6 seconds through nose.
Breathe out for 5-6 seconds through nose or mouth.
Maintain this rhythm for 5-20 minutes.
Keep breathing smooth and effortless.

name: Breath Retention (Kumbhaka)
description: Holding breath to increase lung capacity and mental focus.
steps: Sit in comfortable meditation posture.
Take a deep inhalation.
Retain breath comfortably (start with 5-10 seconds).
Exhale slowly and completely.
Practice with caution and gradually increase retention time.

name: Viloma Pranayama (Against the Wave)
description: Segmented breath practice enhancing control and mindfulness.
steps: Sit comfortably with spine straight.
Inhale in three equal segments with brief pauses.
Exhale smoothly and completely.
Alternatively, inhale smoothly and exhale in segments.
Practice 5-10 rounds of each variation.

name: Lion's Breath (Simhasana)
description: Exaggerated exhale to release tension and energize the body.
steps: Sit on heels or in comfortable cross-legged position.
Place hands on knees with fingers spread wide.
Inhale deeply through nose.
Open mouth wide, stick tongue out toward chin.
Exhale forcefully with 'HAAA' sound while opening eyes wide.
benefits: Releases tension in face and jaw

name: Three-Part Breath (Dirga Pranayama)
description: Sequential filling and emptying of lungs for full expansion.
steps: Lie down or sit comfortably.
Place one hand on belly, one on chest.
Breathe into belly first, then ribcage, then chest.
Exhale in reverse order: chest, ribcage, belly.
Make each part equal in duration.

name: Equal Breathing (Sama Vritti)
description: Matching inhale and exhale durations for balance and calm.
steps: Sit comfortably with spine erect.
Breathe naturally and observe current breath length.
Make inhale and exhale equal in duration.
Start with 4 counts each, gradually increase.
Maintain smooth, steady rhythm.

name: Breath Focus Technique
description: Combines breath awareness with mantra for deeper relaxation.
steps: Choose a calming word or phrase (like 'peace' or 'calm').
Sit or lie in comfortable position.
Breathe naturally and deeply.
Repeat your chosen word/phrase mentally with each exhale.
If mind wanders, gently return focus to breath and word.

name: Chandra Bhedana (Left Nostril Breathing)
description: Left-nostril breathing for parasympathetic activation and calm.
steps: Sit comfortably with spine straight.
Use right thumb to close right nostril.
Inhale slowly and deeply through left nostril only.
Close left nostril with ring finger, release thumb.
Exhale through right nostril.
Repeat for 5-10 rounds.

name: Controlled Coughing/Huffing
description: Technique aiding mucus clearance and improved lung function.
steps: Sit upright in comfortable position.
Take a slow, deep breath filling lungs completely.
Hold breath for 2-3 seconds.
Cough or huff sharply to help clear airways.
Follow with normal breathing.

name: Paced Breathing
description: Consistent rate breathing to regulate stress and heart rate.
steps: Choose a comfortable breathing rate (usually 6-8 breaths per minute).
Use a metronome, app, or counting to maintain pace.
Inhale and exhale at predetermined rhythm.
Focus on maintaining consistent timing.
Practice for 5-20 minutes.

name: Wim Hof Breathing
description: Powerful breaths and retention for resilience and energy boost.
steps: Sit or lie comfortably.
Take 30 deep, powerful breaths (in through nose, out through mouth).
After 30th breath, exhale completely and hold breath as long as comfortable.
When you need to breathe, take deep breath and hold for 15 seconds.
This completes one round; repeat 2-4 rounds.

name: Mindfulness Breathing
description: Nonjudgmental breath observation for present-moment awareness.
steps: Sit comfortably with eyes closed or softly focused.
Breathe naturally without changing the rhythm.
Focus complete attention on breath sensations.
Notice air entering and leaving nostrils.
When mind wanders, gently return attention to breath.

name: Progressive Breathing
description: Gradual deepening of breath to build capacity and relaxation.
steps: Start with normal, natural breathing.
Gradually deepen each breath over several cycles.
Slowly increase the length of inhales and exhales.
Reach comfortable maximum depth and length.
Gradually return to normal breathing.

name: Breath Counting
description: Counting exhales to train focus and quiet the mind.
steps: Sit in comfortable meditation posture.
Breathe naturally and count each exhale.
Count from 1 to 10, then return to 1.
If you lose count, simply start over at 1.
Maintain steady attention on counting and breathing.

name: Kapalabhati (Skull Shining Breath)
description: Rapid skull-shining breaths for lung cleansing and energizing.
steps: Sit tall with spine erect.
Take a deep breath in to prepare.
Forcefully exhale through nose while pulling abdomen in sharply.
Allow passive, natural inhale as abdomen relaxes.
Focus on active exhales; repeat 15-20 cycles.
End with deep inhale, hold briefly, then slow exhale.
